what: Build Geographic Crosswalk Tables for New York City Boundaries
title: Build Geographic Crosswalk Tables for New York City Boundaries
text: nycbuildxwalks downloads official New York City geographic boundary data generates comprehensive crosswalk tables showing administrative spatial boundaries overlap. produces wide-format long-format CSV crosswalks intersection area percentage calculations. R port Python NYC geography crosswalks tool Nathan Storey MODA-NYC, builds earlier work BetaNYC.

title: Build Geographic Crosswalk Tables for New York City Boundaries
text: can install development version nycbuildxwalks GitHub : Alternatively, install package r-universe: Including https://cloud.r-project.org ensures dependencies CRAN resolved automatically.

title: Build Geographic Crosswalk Tables for New York City Boundaries
text: main entry point make_run(), orchestrates full pipeline: can also run individual steps:
code: library(nycbuildxwalks)  # Full run: download boundaries and build all crosswalks run_dir <- make_run()  # With ZIP archives run_dir <- make_run(zip_artifacts = TRUE)  # Test with limited features run_dir <- make_run(max_primaries = 2, primary_only = c("cd", "pp")) # Step 1: Download and combine boundaries run_dir <- download_all_boundaries()  # Step 2: Build crosswalks from existing boundaries build_crosswalks(   boundaries_path = file.path(run_dir, "all_boundaries.geojson"),   run_dir = run_dir )

title: Build Geographic Crosswalk Tables for New York City Boundaries
text: ZIP codes MODZCTAs: zipcode layer uses DOHMH MODZCTA polygons. Results may differ USPS ZIP definitions. Geometry validity: combining inputs, invalid geometries repaired sf::st_make_valid(). Intersection de-noising: small negative buffer (-50 ft default) applied intersection reduce line-touching artifacts. Candidates filtered buffer. intersection, minimum area threshold (100 sq ft) suppresses noise. Multipart primaries: Primary features dissolved name multipart geometries treated single unit. Area calculations: spatial operations use EPSG:2263 (NY State Plane, feet) accurate area computation.

title: Build Geographic Crosswalk Tables for New York City Boundaries
text: run produces timestamped directory outputs/ containing: all_boundaries.geojson — combined boundary file run_meta.json — source URLs, cycle resolutions, git SHA longform/ — one CSV per primary geography pairwise intersection details wide/ — one CSV per primary geography semicolon-separated overlapping feature names crosswalks_meta.json — buffer, thresholds, IDs used

title: Build Geographic Crosswalk Tables for New York City Boundaries
text: package based Python tool Nathan Storey MODA-NYC, builds concepts data aggregation methods originally implemented BetaNYC NYC Boundaries Map repository. Hexlogo Photo: Detail “Brooklyn Paving 5-25-16”, NYC DOT.

what: Build all crosswalk tables from boundary data — build_crosswalks
title: Build all crosswalk tables from boundary data — build_crosswalks
text: Reads combined boundaries GeoJSON file, reprojects EPSG:2263 (NY State Plane, feet) accurate area calculations, builds long-form wide-format crosswalk CSVs primary geography.

title: Build all crosswalk tables from boundary data — build_crosswalks
code: build_crosswalks(   boundaries_path,   run_dir,   buffer_feet = -50,   min_area_final = 100,   epsilon = 1e-06,   exclude_ids = "cc_upcoming",   primary_only = NULL,   targets = NULL,   max_primaries = NULL )

title: Build all crosswalk tables from boundary data — build_crosswalks
text: boundaries_path Path all_boundaries.geojson. run_dir Output directory crosswalk CSVs metadata. buffer_feet Negative buffer feet intersection de-noising. Default -50. min_area_final Minimum intersection area square feet. Default 100. epsilon Tiny area suppress numerical noise. Default 1e-6. exclude_ids Character vector geography IDs exclude. Default "cc_upcoming". primary_only Optional character vector restrict primary geography IDs processed. targets Optional character vector restrict target geography IDs. max_primaries Optional integer limit features per primary (testing).

title: Build all crosswalk tables from boundary data — build_crosswalks
text: path run_dir (invisibly).

what: Build long-form crosswalk for a single primary geography — build_longform_for_primary
title: Build long-form crosswalk for a single primary geography — build_longform_for_primary
text: feature primary geography, computes intersection area every feature target geography. Returns tibble one row per significant pairwise intersection, including area percentage overlap.

title: Build long-form crosswalk for a single primary geography — build_longform_for_primary
code: build_longform_for_primary(   all_gdf,   primary_id,   other_ids,   buffer_feet = -50,   min_area = 100,   epsilon = 1e-06,   max_primaries = NULL )

title: Build long-form crosswalk for a single primary geography — build_longform_for_primary
text: all_gdf sf object containing boundaries, projected EPSG:2263 (NY State Plane, feet). primary_id geography ID use primary (e.g., "cd"). other_ids Character vector target geography IDs. buffer_feet Negative buffer (feet) applied intersection suppress edge artifacts. Default -50. min_area Minimum intersection area square feet. Default 100. epsilon Tiny area threshold suppress numerical noise. Default 1e-6. max_primaries Optional integer limit number primary features processed (useful testing).

title: Build long-form crosswalk for a single primary geography — build_longform_for_primary
text: tibble columns primary_geo_id, primary_geo_name, other_geo_id, other_geo_name, primary_area_sqft, intersection_area_sqft, pct_overlap.

what: Build wide-format crosswalk for a single primary geography — build_wide_for_primary
title: Build wide-format crosswalk for a single primary geography — build_wide_for_primary
text: feature primary geography, finds overlapping features target geographies returns tibble one row per primary feature one column per target geography (values semicolon-separated names overlapping features).

title: Build wide-format crosswalk for a single primary geography — build_wide_for_primary
code: build_wide_for_primary(   all_gdf,   primary_id,   other_ids,   buffer_feet = -50,   min_area = 100,   epsilon = 1e-06,   max_primaries = NULL )

title: Build wide-format crosswalk for a single primary geography — build_wide_for_primary
text: all_gdf sf object containing boundaries, projected EPSG:2263 (NY State Plane, feet). primary_id geography ID use primary (e.g., "cd"). other_ids Character vector target geography IDs. buffer_feet Negative buffer (feet) applied intersection suppress edge artifacts. Default -50. min_area Minimum intersection area square feet. Default 100. epsilon Tiny area threshold suppress numerical noise. Default 1e-6. max_primaries Optional integer limit number primary features processed (useful testing).

title: Build wide-format crosswalk for a single primary geography — build_wide_for_primary
text: tibble one row per primary feature. first column named primary_id contains primary feature names. Remaining columns named target geography ID contain semicolon-separated overlapping feature names.

what: Dissolve features by name column — dissolve_by_name
title: Dissolve features by name column — dissolve_by_name
text: Groups features name_col unions geometries, producing one feature per unique name. prevents duplicate rows multipart features (e.g., MODZCTAs).

title: Dissolve features by name column — dissolve_by_name
Dissolve features by name column — dissolve_by_name
text: gdf sf object columns id, name_col, geometry.

title: Dissolve features by name column — dissolve_by_name
text: sf object one row per unique name_col value, columns id, name_col, dissolved geometry.

what: Download and combine all NYC boundary datasets — download_all_boundaries
title: Download and combine all NYC boundary datasets — download_all_boundaries
text: Iterates datasets defined nyc_datasets, downloads one via process_dataset(), combines single sf object, fixes invalid geometries, writes results timestamped output directory.

title: Download and combine all NYC boundary datasets — download_all_boundaries
code: download_all_boundaries(   output_dir = "outputs",   auto_detect_latest = TRUE,   preferred_cycle = NULL,   external_data_dir = "data/external",   datasets = NULL )

title: Download and combine all NYC boundary datasets — download_all_boundaries
text: output_dir Base directory timestamped run outputs. Default "outputs". auto_detect_latest Logical. Attempt auto-detect latest DCP cycle letter. Default TRUE. preferred_cycle Optional single letter pin DCP cycle. external_data_dir Path local fallback files. Default "data/external". datasets tibble dataset definitions. Defaults nyc_datasets.

title: Download and combine all NYC boundary datasets — download_all_boundaries
text: path timestamped run directory (invisibly).

what: Run the full crosswalk generation pipeline — make_run
title: Run the full crosswalk generation pipeline — make_run
text: Orchestrates complete workflow: downloads NYC boundary datasets via download_all_boundaries(), builds crosswalk tables via build_crosswalks(). Optionally creates ZIP archives outputs.

title: Run the full crosswalk generation pipeline — make_run
code: make_run(   output_dir = "outputs",   auto_detect_latest = TRUE,   preferred_cycle = NULL,   external_data_dir = "data/external",   buffer_feet = -50,   min_area_final = 100,   epsilon = 1e-06,   exclude_ids = "cc_upcoming",   primary_only = NULL,   targets = NULL,   max_primaries = NULL,   zip_artifacts = FALSE )

title: Run the full crosswalk generation pipeline — make_run
text: output_dir Base directory outputs. Default "outputs". auto_detect_latest Logical. Auto-detect latest DCP cycle. Default TRUE. preferred_cycle Optional DCP cycle letter pin. external_data_dir Path local fallback files. Default "data/external". buffer_feet Negative buffer intersection de-noising. Default -50. min_area_final Minimum intersection area (sq ft). Default 100. epsilon Numerical noise threshold. Default 1e-6. exclude_ids Geography IDs exclude. Default "cc_upcoming". primary_only Optional character vector primary IDs build. targets Optional character vector target IDs. max_primaries Optional limit features per primary (testing). zip_artifacts Logical. TRUE, create ZIP archives outputs. Default FALSE.

title: Run the full crosswalk generation pipeline — make_run
text: path timestamped run directory (invisibly).

title: NYC geographic dataset definitions — nyc_datasets
text: tibble containing metadata 15 NYC geographic boundary datasets used build crosswalk tables. row defines dataset's source URL, column used feature names, source type.

title: NYC geographic dataset definitions — nyc_datasets
NYC geographic dataset definitions — nyc_datasets
text: tibble 15 rows 6 columns: id Short identifier geography (e.g., "cd", "pp", "nta") dataset_name Human-readable name geography url Source URL downloading boundary data name_col Name column source data contains feature names name_alt Optional alternate name column, NA none source_type One "dcp_zip" (DCP shapefile zip cycle detection), "opendata_shapefile" (NYC Open Data shapefile export), "opendata_geojson" (NYC Open Data GeoJSON), "edc_zip" (EDC shapefile zip)

title: NYC geographic dataset definitions — nyc_datasets
text: NYC Department City Planning (DCP), NYC Open Data, NYC Economic Development Corporation (EDC). Dataset definitions adapted Python tool Nathan Storey MODA-NYC (https://github.com/MODA-NYC/nyc-geography-crosswalks).

what: nycbuildxwalks: Build Geographic Crosswalk Tables for New York City Boundaries — nycbuildxwalks-package
title: nycbuildxwalks: Build Geographic Crosswalk Tables for New York City Boundaries — nycbuildxwalks-package
text: Downloads official New York City geographic boundary data NYC Open Data, Department City Planning, sources, generates comprehensive crosswalk tables showing administrative spatial boundaries overlap. Produces wide-format long-format CSV crosswalks intersection area percentage calculations. Based Python tool Nathan Storey MODA-NYC (https://github.com/MODA-NYC/nyc-geography-crosswalks), builds earlier work BetaNYC (https://github.com/BetaNYC/nyc-boundaries).

title: nycbuildxwalks: Build Geographic Crosswalk Tables for New York City Boundaries — nycbuildxwalks-package
text: nycbuildxwalks downloads official NYC geographic boundary data generates crosswalk tables showing administrative boundaries overlap. main entry point make_run(), orchestrates full pipeline. package R port Python tool Nathan Storey MODA-NYC (https://github.com/MODA-NYC/nyc-geography-crosswalks), builds earlier work BetaNYC (https://github.com/BetaNYC/nyc-boundaries).

title: nycbuildxwalks: Build Geographic Crosswalk Tables for New York City Boundaries — nycbuildxwalks-package
text: Maintainer: Kieran Healy kjhealy@gmail.com (ORCID) Authors: Nathan Storey (Author original Python implementation)

what: Download and standardize a single NYC boundary dataset — process_dataset
title: Download and standardize a single NYC boundary dataset — process_dataset
text: Downloads geographic boundary dataset, reads sf object, reprojects EPSG:4326, standardizes columns common schema (id, name_col, optionally name_alt, geometry).

title: Download and standardize a single NYC boundary dataset — process_dataset
code: process_dataset(   dataset_info,   auto_detect_latest = TRUE,   preferred_cycle = NULL,   external_data_dir = "data/external" )

title: Download and standardize a single NYC boundary dataset — process_dataset
text: dataset_info single-row tibble list elements id, dataset_name, url, name_col, name_alt, source_type. auto_detect_latest Logical. Passed resolve_dcp_cycle() DCP zip sources. Default TRUE. preferred_cycle Optional cycle letter passed resolve_dcp_cycle(). external_data_dir Path directory containing local fallback files (e.g., ibz.zip). Default "data/external".

title: Download and standardize a single NYC boundary dataset — process_dataset
text: list components: gdf: sf object standardized columns, NULL failure. meta: list id, original_url, resolved_url, cycle, auto_detected, status, error.

what: Resolve the latest available DCP cycle for a URL — resolve_dcp_cycle
title: Resolve the latest available DCP cycle for a URL — resolve_dcp_cycle
text: DCP boundary files versioned cycle suffix (e.g., _25a.zip). function probes newer cycles sending HTTP HEAD requests ascending letters (b, c, d, ...) returns URL highest available cycle.

title: Resolve the latest available DCP cycle for a URL — resolve_dcp_cycle
Resolve the latest available DCP cycle for a URL — resolve_dcp_cycle
text: url DCP boundary URL containing cycle suffix like _25a.zip. auto_detect Logical. TRUE (default), probe newer cycles. preferred_cycle Optional single lowercase letter pin specific cycle (e.g., "d"). set available, overrides auto-detection.

title: Resolve the latest available DCP cycle for a URL — resolve_dcp_cycle
text: list components: resolved_url: URL best available cycle. meta: list cycle_source, cycle_resolved, auto_detected, probes.

what: Union features by name column — union_by_name
title: Union features by name column — union_by_name
text: Groups features name_col unions group's geometry single geometry. Returns tibble name-geometry pairs rather sf object, use intersection calculations.

title: Union features by name column — union_by_name
Union features by name column — union_by_name
text: gdf sf object name_col column.

title: Union features by name column — union_by_name
text: tibble columns name (character) geometry (sfc_GEOMETRY).

title: nycbuildxwalks (development version)
text: Initial package creation. Port Python NYC geography crosswalks tool Nathan Storey MODA-NYC. Added nyc_datasets tibble definitions 15 NYC geographic boundary datasets. Added download_all_boundaries() download combine boundary data official NYC sources. Added build_crosswalks() generate long-form wide-format crosswalk CSVs boundary data. Added make_run() orchestrate full pipeline.
